Provisional Irish Republican Army.description: Irish republican paramilitary force that sought to end British rule in Northern Ireland and create an independent socialist republic. It emerged in December 1969 from a split in the IRA and was the most active republican group during the Troubles. The IRA used guerrilla tactics and bombings, killing over 1,700 people. It declared a ceasefire in 1997 and formally ended its campaign in 2005.

Cártel de Jalisco Nueva Generación.description: Mexican criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking and arms trafficking, designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the United States and Argentina. Founded in 2007 as an armed wing under the name Los Mata Zetas, it became independent in 2010 and emerged publicly in September 2011. Led by Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes (El Mencho) from 2009 until his death in 2026, it is considered one of the most dangerous criminal groups in Mexico and a rival of the Sinaloa Cartel.

Buffalo crime family.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Buffalo, New York, founded around 1910 by Angelo Palmeri. It operates throughout Western New York, Erie, Pennsylvania, and Hamilton, Ontario, Canada. The family was led for decades by Stefano Magaddino, a member of The Commission, and is currently headed by Joseph A. Todaro Jr. It is involved in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, drug trafficking, and other illegal activities.

Originally focused on uniting white power skinheads across Pennsylvania, it expanded to multiple chapters and became one of the largest single-state racist skinhead crews in the US. Cartel de Medellín.description: The Medellín Cartel was a loose coalition of Colombian drug trafficking organizations based in Medellín, Colombia, that played a central role in the international cocaine trade during the late 1970s and 1980s. It was founded by Pablo Escobar, Carlos Lehder, Gonzalo Rodríguez Gacha, and the Ochoa brothers. The network was known for its violence, including terrorism and assassinations, and was designated a terrorist group by the United States. It was dismantled in the early 1990s following the death of Escobar.

La Nueva Familia Michoacana.description: Mexican criminal organization specializing in drug trafficking, illegal mining, and extortion. Formed in 2011 as a splinter group after the original La Familia Michoacana lost power. Led by brothers Johnny and José Alfredo Hurtado Olascoaga. Operates primarily in Michoacán, Guerrero, and the State of Mexico, and is involved in distributing cocaine, fentanyl, and methamphetamine to the United States. Designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the U.S. State Department in 2025.

North Preston's Finest.description: A gang of pimps based in North Preston, Nova Scotia, Canada, founded in the mid-to-late 1980s. The gang is involved in pimping, weapons and drug trafficking, money laundering, and murder. It is the most prominent of approximately ten gangs in the Halifax Regional Municipality, with most members being Black Canadians from North Preston.

211 Crew.description: American white supremacist prison gang formed in 1995 at Denver County Jail by Benjamin Davis. Active both in and out of prison, it is involved in murder, weapons and drug trafficking, kidnapping, and robbery. The gang has been linked to high-profile murders including that of Colorado Department of Corrections head Tom Clements. Its membership is estimated at 1,000.

Corleonesi.description: The Corleonesi was an alliance of Mafia families within the Sicilian Mafia, formed in the 1970s. Led by Luciano Leggio, Salvatore Riina, Bernardo Provenzano, and others, it took over the Sicilian Mafia Commission and imposed a dictatorship over Cosa Nostra. The coalition waged the Second Mafia War from 1978–1983 and dominated until 2006.
